title: rumex acetosella var. angiocarpus
intro: Rumex acetosella var. angiocarpus, also known as red sorrel or red sheep's sorrel, is a type of perennial plant belonging to the buckwheat family. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its high content of oxalic acid and ascorbic acid, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rumex acetosella var. angiocarpus include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the leaves and flowers to make a tincture or salve, although it is essential to note that excessive consumption can cause kidney damage and other health issues.
